name: Cummings v. Premier Rehab Keller, P.L.L.C.
summary: The Court held that emotional distress damages are not recoverable under the Rehabilitation Act or Affordable Care Act because such damages were not traditionally available in breach of contract suits.
significance: Applied the contract analogy to hold that emotional distress damages are not available under Spending Clause antidiscrimination statutes because a reasonable funding recipient would not have understood they were agreeing to such liability. Respondents rely on its emphasis on bilateral agreement.
